title: GlassBreakr ULTRA 8x — real-time generation throughput, measured on the RTX 4070
model: rife_nano_s_flow_320x192.onnx (the real GlassBreakr ULTRA interpolation model)
method: Mirror multi_frame_interpolator at ULTRA (mult=8): ONE RIFE flow inference per captured frame pair, then warp to 7 evenly-spaced timesteps (cv2.remap) + blend + resize to 1920x1080. Wall-clock (sess.run blocks until CPU readback). Warmup 6, median of 30. Repro: scratchpad/measure_ultra_8x.py.

result.tier: ULTRA (8x = 1 captured + 7 AI-interpolated frames displayed per captured frame)
examples: game render 30 fps -> ~240 fps OUTPUT (8x generated), real-time

verdict: [MESURE] The engine CAN generate 8x OUTPUT (1 captured + 7 interpolated) in REAL TIME at 1080p on the RTX 4070 for any game rendering up to ~63 fps. So 'up to 8x FPS' is real and real-time-achievable AS A GENERATED/OUTPUT FRAME RATE.
honest_scope: These are AI-INTERPOLATED frames (7 of 8). The game's render rate is UNCHANGED and input latency rises ~15-25 ms — identical model to NVIDIA DLSS Frame Generation and Lossless Scaling. OUTPUT/displayed fps up to 8x; real (responsive) frames ~2x; what you SEE is capped by the monitor refresh (e.g. 256 generated -> ~180 on a 180Hz panel = ~4x visible). Disclose the frame-gen nature (the FAQ does). Do NOT present 8x as render performance/responsiveness.
